title: Add support for Overlay volumes into the container.
body:
Overlay mounts allow buildah bud and buildah from to
specify a directory on the disk that will be mounted
as an overlay into the container, where the overlay can be written to
but when the RUN or buildah run exits, the modified files will dissapear.

The basic idea is to be able to mount cache from the disk for things like yum/dnf/apt
to be able to be used and modified in the contianer on a run command, but to be
kept fresh for each RUN.

Signed-off-by: Daniel J Walsh <dwalsh@redhat.com>

comments.body: :umbrella: The latest upstream changes (presumably d3c85f4) made this pull request unmergeable. Please resolve the merge conflicts.

:pushpin: Commit cde3cf3 has been approved by `giuseppe`

<!-- @rh-atomic-bot r=giuseppe cde3cf3d81b2f12f883978a95bdecaeb817bb35f -->
:hourglass: Testing commit cde3cf3 with merge bcc5e51...

What happens when the underlying directory changes after the overlay is created? What happens if both the underlying and overlaid directories change?

Edit: I should mention that I love the feature!

:sunny: Test successful - [status-papr](https://s3.amazonaws.com/aos-ci/ghprb/projectatomic/buildah/bcc5e51a948c1847fab1c932f1a343696a96cafd.1558357043266997760/index.html), [status-travis](https://travis-ci.org/containers/buildah/builds/534810107?utm_source=github_status&utm_medium=notification)
Approved by: giuseppe
Pushing bcc5e51a948c1847fab1c932f1a343696a96cafd to master...

If underlying directory is changed, it is undefined.  That is covered in the man page.
Overlaid directory changing does not matter, since the changes will end up temporarily in the upper directory.
